intro_executive_summary: RBI Group, a traditional bank serving clients across multiple markets with ~7.5 million retail customers, aims to stay competitive in consumer payments. Visa made a proactive offer in FY25 to drive and support early adoption of Visa Direct to card (OCT & AFT) and Visa+ origination for consumer usecases like remittance and top ups. RBI has since provided an updated forecast and requested increased fixed support for development in FY26 and commercial rollout in FY27. The renewed proposal is designed to meet client demand, lock in solution launches across markets, drive XB performance. With this contract we'd win volume from and grow against Wise, Paypal, WeRo etc.
intro_alignment_with_ve_corporate_goals: This approach aligns with Visa's strategic corporate goals and FY26 / FY27 scorecard by securing Visa Direct's origination penetration in the banking segment and expanding the adoption of Visa Direct value-added services such as Visa+,  driving improved user experiences and driving usage.  Additionally, selling Visa Direct (to Card) supports Visa's goal to diversify revenues beyond traditional consumer payments.
intro_win_retain_strategy: Following Visa’s proactive FY25 offer, RBI expanded the scope to include domestic flows, revised the XB forecast, and requested higher fixed support committing to centralised development in FY26 and commercial rollout across countries in FY27 of OCT, AFT and Visa+ origination. Visa aims to secure contracting in FY26 and targets funding the fixed support through a mix of Visa Direct and CIA card‑related incentives, supported by coordinated C‑level to operational engagement. This groupdeal approach empowers RBI at central level, which is something Visa and key RBI staeholders look to establish since long.
intro_key_benefits: Reasons for approval: this offer fits within Visa Direct fast track modelling KPI's and fast track contracting. Visa benefits are commited commercial rollout of OCT, AFT and Visa+ products with a key bank across multiple markets and growing & winning volumes against competitors like Wise.  The offer stimulates XB performance through tiered transactional XB support and enhances focus on implementation and activation through milestone based fixed support that protects Visa's interests.
intro_key_commercial_concerns: RBI has an remittance partnership with Wise in some markets. Not approving this deal will risk volumes going to Wise. One senior stakeholder is known to have a appreciation for Wise, which may influence internal discussions.  In addition, RBI’s organisational complexity and resource constraints—similar to other large group banks such as Erste or ING—create to be expected risks in eg execution including possible shifts in timelines or scope despite Visa’s support.  Tight project governance and continued alignment on scope, timelines, and resourcing will be essential to mitigate delivery risks and avoid delays.
intro_other_factors: RBI’s geographical scale offers strong strategic upside potential for Visa, This contract will have an signal effect to other bankingclients like Erste Bank, ING. As RBI internal rules currently do not allow to do XB P2P card based payments into all XB corridors - once lifted this could lead to increased inter levels. However, capturing this deal and value requires careful navigation of their multi‑layered decision processes and expectations around support.

file_name: Speedy - Visa Direct - Latest Pricing vJan26 AuoContract v2.xlsm
intro_executive_summary: Speedy is a high growth Acquirer owned by Centrue, recognised by the Financial Times as one of Europe's fastest growing companies. Speedy (EU) and Financial House (UK) are related entites, both owned by Centrue. Speedy is looking for support to accelerate their payouts and remittance volumes for their merchants, which includes Jeton, a remitter also owned by the Centrue group.   Speedy has a network of existing merchants who they also want to roll out FD use-cases too.
intro_alignment_with_ve_corporate_goals: Speedy wants Visa Direct to accelerate their XB money movement business, which aligns with Visa's XB growth strategy
intro_win_retain_strategy: To win the deal, Visa would look to help the client integrate and launch their solution, as well as provide a volume based discount on XB OCTs. The client has flagged that as they have an existing book of merchants, they do not require an upfront or VIK, and would instead prefer to receive the volume based XB discount instead.
intro_key_benefits: Unlocks a Remitter, as well as  merchant clients.  Speedy currently processes payouts via SEPA, SEPA Instant and SWIFT. Estimated current volume is 3.5 - 4.0 million transactions over the past 12 months, with an average value of EUR150 domestic / EUR120 intra EEA. Jeton has processed an average 3.5 - 4.0 million remittance payouts in the same period.
intro_key_commercial_concerns: Low risk opportunity, with established remitter client to help drive volumes. Client is incentivised from Day 1 to drive volumes.
intro_other_factors: The client wants to have an identical deal to Financial House, with the same start date and condition, so that they can ensure they can manage both consistently.
intro_client_full_name: SPEEDY AG SPOLKA Z OGRANICZONA ODPOWIEDZIALNOSCIA

file_name: TkPay_VDC Pricing - Latest Pricing vJan26 AuoContract -07Apr26_V6_AfterCMSDR.xlsm

intro_executive_summary: Checkout is bringing to Visa a list of 15 new merchants representing an annual incremental PV of €55BN for Visa Checkout has provided information on volumes from existing merchants that Visa does not capture today, representing an annual incremental PV of €29BN for Visa.
intro_alignment_with_ve_corporate_goals: Increase PV volumes on card rails - Capture incremental PV and net revenues for Visa.
intro_win_retain_strategy: Winning new business with CKO and Liquid Assets merchants Retaining key liquid assets merchants such as Interactive Investors whose volumes are declining (-46% from 2025 to 2024)
intro_key_benefits: Incremental PV / Net revenues for Visa and issuers (on new merchants to card rails) Supporting CKO to retain volumes on card rails with key merchants moving away from card rails because of AFT Pricing uncapped in that sector

file_name: Visa Direct - Latest Pricing vJan26 AuoContract 1.xlsm
Visa Direct - Latest Pricing vJan26 AuoContract_ING_VDC_v2.xlsm
intro_executive_summary: ING Germany is a high-priority Visa Direct opportunity. Since summer 2025, our engagement on Visa+, OCT mandates, and Visa Direct has positioned VD as a strategic lever for ING’s payments and card agenda. Cross-border retail payments are now a board-level priority, driven by pressure from Revolut, Wise, and other disruptors and growing customer leakage. Visa Direct is resonating as a complement to Wero and traditional correspondent banking, extending ING’s proposition into cross-border consumer remittances, where it is currently behind the market. Momentum is building, with ING preparing an internal decision paper and evaluating VDC, VDA, and VDW options. For VDC WOrldline is considered as enabler.
intro_alignment_with_ve_corporate_goals: This opportunity aligns strongly with Visa’s corporate goals and FY26/FY27 scorecard priorities by increasing Visa Direct origination penetration in the banking segment and expanding adoption of value-added services such as Visa+, which enhance user experience and stimulate transaction growth. Visa Direct to Card also supports Visa’s broader objective to diversify revenue beyond traditional consumer payments, while strengthening Visa’s position in strategic cross-border and account-based money movement use cases.
intro_win_retain_strategy: ING diba requested indicative pricing, signalling credible buying intent. Our response must be formal, commercially sharp, and execution-focused to secure local approval. This is relevant beyond DE: ING Romania & Poland are live with OCT origination, ING NL is interested. ING HQ may move toward a centralized remittance RFP. Given the limited local VIK availability, we should consider targeted Visa Direct VIK support, on top of the XB rebate, rather than an earnout, to create a strong local buying intend as well as a simpler offer, while linking fixed support to tangible execution milestones (implemenation, activation). Local Visa sponsorship is strong, with RMD and CM DE engaged, and a parallel VDA/VDW offer is being prepared.
intro_key_benefits: 1. sizeable opportunity with healthy KPIs and credible buying intent. 2. Simpler, more effective commercial structure: a CMSDR model with fixed support plus XB rebate makes the offer more tangible, improve local attractiveness, and increase the likelihood of approval in DE. Closing the German deal before the potantial central RFP is issued is in Visa's interest.  3. Strategic client win in Germany: landing ING Germany would establish Visa Direct with a major direct bank and strengthen our position with other German banks reviewing their XB payments strategy. 4. Broader ING upside: winning locally would improve our chances of a wider ING Group opportunity.
intro_key_commercial_concerns: 1. Group-level dynamics may influence local decision-making, particularly if ING HQ moves toward a broader or centralized remittance approach. 2. Offer design will be critical: if our proposal is too complex or not attractive enough commercially, we risk losing momentum at the local approval stage. 3. Limited local VIK / resouce availability may constrain flexibility and require targeted fixed support to make the business case work for ING Germany. 4. Competitive pressure is real, ING will also be assessing  alternative providers and solution models(MCI, potentially Wise, Citi) , which could affect pricing and structure.
intro_other_factors: This opportunity has both local urgency and broader group significance: ING Romania and Poland are already live with OCT origination, ING Netherlands has expressed interest, and ING HQ is becoming increasingly aware of the urgency, our value proposition, and their country-level progress—raising the prospect of a centralized RFP on consumer remittances. Strong support from RMD and CM Germany, including board-level socialization and C-level engagement, gives us a solid platform. We should treat this German opportunity as a strategically important deal for CE as entry point for a subsequent group deal that impacts CE and CEE, requiring sharp pricing, proactive support, and thoughtful handling of enablement/VIK for DE.
